results.title: Space design apps (2026): Compare the Best | Product Hunt
results.content:
# Space design apps (2026): Compare the Best

Space design apps help plan rooms and workplaces using 3D, AR, and AI. They map spaces, test layouts, pick furniture, and support hybrid offices and home makeovers.

Kinopio
Dymaxion
Coohom
Archonet
Denote
Desky
Fin 
Promoted

## Top reviewed space design apps [...] ## Top reviewed space design apps

Drafted
Gaia
Room Scanner
Imed Radhouani
Rankfender

Rankfender [...] The pattern is clear: shipping AI as a new app is the slow path. The fast path is grafting onto a surface the user already touches. The cost of building a standalone AI app dropped 90%+. The cost of getting it noticed did not. Surface integration sidesteps the noticing problem because the surface already has users.

Sean Reid
Deconflict

Deconflict

Big update. The optimizer can now prioritize high-traffic rooms over hallways and closets. Shoutout to @yotam\_dahan who suggested density-aware placement.

What do you think? … [...] Marek Miltner
PangeAI

PangeAI

@cremer PangeAI = geospatial AI. No consensus problem here, but we do have the "valid in WGS84, cursed in EPSG:3857" problem. Solidarity. 🌎🫶

Debojyoti Singha
Keplars

Keplars

#### What's great

Most AI agents understand data but not the real world, and that gap is huge in geospatial. PangeAI seems to bridge that nicely, especially with practical use cases like querying real-world events without a full GIS setup.

Feels genuinely useful, not just “AI for the hype.” Curious to see how it scales. [...] Marek Miltner
PangeAI

PangeAI

Yan Bin, Pan
PangeAI

PangeAI

Most AI agents know what they’re talking about, but they have no idea where they are. We’re here to make sure the robots don’t try to take a shortcut through a lake just because it's a straight line on a CSV

Please get in touch with us for info, we'd love to map things for you :)

Marek Miltner
PangeAI

PangeAI

Gerardus Cremer

@yanbin\_pan hi!  
  
the "where" problem gets even weirder when you need consensus on it.  
we have been building an on-chian land registry where the hard part is not intelligence, it is getting a blockchain to agree on polygon intersections without any oracles..  
different stack, same enemy: geometry that looks fine until it suddenly isn't!

Marek Miltner
PangeAI

PangeAI
